item.title: About CrySPY
item.description: About CrySPY CrySPY is a crystal structure prediction tool written in Python, distributed under the MIT License. Table of contents Crystal structure prediction Searching algorithms Interface Logo

item.title: Version information
item.description: Version information Table of contents Version 1.1.0 Version 1.0.0 Version 0.10.3 or earlier

item.title: Installation
item.description: Installation Note You need (CrySPY + Python environment + structure optimizer) in your workstation, super computer, etc. Table of contents System requirements Python Structure optimizer find_wy (optional) CrySPY CrySPY 1.0.0 or later CrySPY 0.10.3 or earlier CrySPY utility (optional)

item.title: Tutorial
item.description: Tutorial Info You can find examaple files in cryspy_utility/examples. Table of contents Random Search (RS) soiap in your local PC VASP QE OpenMX LAMMPS External program Check cryspy.in Script to run Firsrt run Submit job Check results Append structures Analysis and visualization Load external data Evolutionary Algorithm (EA) Bayesian Optimization (BO) LAQA Molecular crystal structure prediction Random structure generation with MPI

item.title: Seaching algorithms
item.description: Searching algorithms Table of contents Random search (RS) Evolutionary algorithm (EA) Bayesian optimizaion (BO) LAQA

item.title: Structure generation
item.description: Structure generation CrySPY currently has three random structure generation modes: crystal (default), mol, and mol_bs. PyXtal (or find_wy) is used for the structure generation. Table of contents struc_mode = crystal struc_mode = mol struc_mode = mol_bs

item.title: Features
item.description: Features Table of contents Backup Clean Restriction on interatomic distances CrySPY_ID in job files Structure generation with MPI parallelization

item.title: Input file
item.description: Input file Description of the input file, cryspy.in. Table of contents File format [basic] section [structure] section [VASP] section [QE] section [OMX] section [soaip] section [LAMMPS] section [EA] section [BO] section [LAQA] section [option] section

item.title: Data format
item.description: Data format All pickle data are stored in the ./data/pkl_data/ directory. Table of contents Common data Initial and optimized structure data Result data Random Search (RS) Evolutionary algorithm (EA) Bayesian Optimization (BO) LAQA Optional data Energy step data Structure step data Force step data Stress step data

item.title: CrySPY Utility
item.description: CrySPY Utility See Installation/CrySPY utility to download. Some (but not all) examples can also be downloaded from this document site. Table of contents Examples soiap_Si8_RS soiap_Si8_RS_mindist qe_Si8_RS qe_benzene_2_RS_mol qe_Si16_LAQA extract_struc.py pos2pkl.py kpt_check.py
